verdict_reason: 4 critical issues found: missing notification_type enum values, duplicate notification insert with wrong type in conflict resolve, retry policy not wired to queue defaults, and LOW_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD set to 0.40 instead of 0.70. Additionally 5 high-severity issues including missing attestation action column, conflict case insert race condition, and malformed Grok response caching.

all_issues.description: notification_type DB enum missing 'conflict_resolved'. publishNotification silently maps 'conflict_resolved' → 'conflict_detected' via typeMap, making resolution and detection notifications indistinguishable in the DB. A migration to add 'conflict_resolved' to the notification_type enum is required.

Duplicate notification insertion: inserts a notification row with type 'conflict_detected' inside the withTenant transaction, THEN calls publishNotification() after commit which also inserts a notification row. Creates two notification rows per resolve action. The in-transaction insert also uses wrong type 'conflict_detected' instead of plan-required 'conflict_resolved'.
all_issues.plan_reference: api_endpoints[1].implementation_notes: 'insert notification type conflict_resolved … Publish notification after transaction commit'

all_issues.description: semanticConflictQueue does not set attempts or backoff in defaultJobOptions. SEMANTIC_CONFLICT_RETRY_POLICY constant is defined in the worker file but never wired into queue definition. Additionally, src/lib/observations/transitions.ts enqueues with attempts:2/delay:1000 instead of plan-required attempts:3/backoff:2s/4s/8s.
all_issues.plan_reference: background_jobs.error_handling[0]: 'BullMQ retries: 3 attempts with exponential backoff 2s, 4s, 8s'

all_issues.description: LOW_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD = 0.40 in code but plan specifies 0.70. All plan documents and existing tests (slice2-constants.test.ts, slice2-ocr-parse.test.ts) assert 0.70. Tests will fail against current value.

attestation_records schema has no 'action' column. Plan requires storing submitted|approved action per attestation, but no column exists and no migration was added. attestationAction variable is computed but never used (dead code).

Conflict case insert uses SELECT-then-INSERT pattern instead of atomic INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING. Under concurrent workers processing the same pair, both can SELECT 0 rows and INSERT, creating duplicate conflict cases. Plan requires ON CONFLICT/duplicate guard for atomicity.
all_issues.plan_reference: background_jobs.error_handling[4]: 'Conflict case insert races are handled via ON CONFLICT/duplicate guard'

all_issues.description: Malformed Grok classifier responses are unconditionally cached as DIFFERENT_KEY. Plan states 'do not write cache entry' for malformed output. A malformed response cached permanently prevents valid re-classification on future jobs.
all_issues.plan_reference: background_jobs.error_handling[3]: 'Malformed classifier output: log parse error, do not write cache entry, treat as DIFFERENT_KEY'

all_issues.description: notification_type enum missing 'pipeline_done'. Plan specifies this type but DB has 'pipeline_completed'. publishNotification maps 'pipeline_done' → 'pipeline_completed' silently, creating inconsistency between SSE payload type and persisted DB type.

ConflictDetectedEvent and NotificationEvent types defined in src/lib/pipeline/events.ts instead of plan-specified src/app/api/pipeline/events/route.ts. Types also include extra 'id: number' field not in plan definitions.
No server-side initial fetch to GET /api/conflicts. All data fetching done client-side in ConflictInboxPage via useEffect, causing loading flash on initial render.
Zod schema (manualObservationSchema) defined but never connected to react-hook-form via zodResolver. useForm uses no resolver. Zod schema is effectively dead code.
categoryId field defined in Zod schema but no UI input rendered for selecting a category.

Raw SQL with 'FROM observation o' returns snake_case columns that don't match Drizzle-inferred camelCase types. Consumer in semanticConflict.ts uses fragile dual-access patterns like '(obsB as any).observation_id ?? (obsB as any).observationId'.

Key equivalence cache hash not company-scoped. buildCacheKey uses only keyA/keyB, not companyId. Different companies with same key pair could share cache entries, leaking cross-tenant classification results.

Worker duplicates notification insert + Redis pub/sub logic inline instead of calling publishNotification() from src/lib/notifications/publish.ts as plan requires. Bypasses utility's type-mapping and unread count logic.
Worker imports publishPipelineEvent but never calls it — publishes directly via redis.publish(). Bypasses centralized event publishing logic.
conflictGroupId always generated as new UUIDv7. Plan says it should reuse existing group when related conflicts share a normalized key.
all_issues.plan_reference: background_jobs.steps[4]: 'conflict_group_id (existing from candidate group or new UUIDv7)'

all_issues.description: Returns undocumented 422 VALIDATION_ERROR code for invalid UUID format and reason length. Plan error_responses only lists 422 OBSERVATION_NOT_IN_CONFLICT.
Plan references document_version.uploaded_at but schema has created_at. Semantically equivalent but naming mismatch could cause runtime errors if implemented literally.
Exported constant name is QUEUE_TIMEOUT_MS (singular) but plan specifies QUEUE_TIMEOUTS_MS (plural).
No upper bound enforced on 'limit' query parameter. Client could pass limit=100000 and trigger unbounded database query.
all_issues.plan_reference: api_endpoints[0].validation_rules: 'page and limit must be positive integers'

all_issues.description: TODO comments present in semanticConflict.ts, publish.ts, notifications/[id]/read/route.ts, and observations/manual/route.ts.
Missing user_reviewed filter option. Only auto_resolved and user_overridden are mapped in the resolution status filter.
EventSource connects to /api/pipeline/events without explicit credential handling. May not send cookies in all deployment configurations.
all_issues.plan_reference: files[17].sections[0]: 'Subscribe to existing pipeline SSE stream'

all_issues.description: Dead code: attestationAction variable computed as 'approved'|'submitted' but never referenced in any insert or audit log.
Numeric tolerance check: when both values are 0, guard on numA !== 0 skips check entirely, treating identical zero-valued observations as a conflict.
No value comparison for non-numeric observations. Two observations with identical text values classified as SAME_KEY would produce a spurious conflict.
Module-level singleton Redis connection via getRedis() is never closed on worker shutdown, leaking the connection.
No .env.example file exists for developer onboarding. All 6 env vars are validated in src/lib/env.ts but undiscoverable without reading code.
Uses process.env.REDIS_URL directly instead of centralized env validation from src/lib/env.ts. Missing REDIS_URL would produce opaque error.
QUEUE_TIMEOUT_MS includes undocumented 'agent-loop: 0' entry not in plan's 6-entry value specification.

acceptance_criteria_results.criterion: Approving two observations with same normalized_key and overlapping period creates ConflictCase with auto_resolved=true and later timestamp winner.
acceptance_criteria_results.evidence: findCandidatePairs filters by period overlap. semanticConflict.ts detects exact key match. latestWins picks later timestamp. Conflict case inserted with autoResolved: true and winningObservationId = winner.id.

acceptance_criteria_results.criterion: POST /api/conflicts/{id}/resolve with valid chosenObservationId returns 200, creates ConflictResolution, supersedes loser, and marks affected DerivationResult rows stale.
acceptance_criteria_results.evidence: resolve/route.ts inserts conflict_resolution, sets loser status to superseded, marks derivation_result rows stale, returns 200. All in withTenant transaction.
acceptance_criteria_results.notes: Derivation staleness applied to ALL observations in conflict, not just loser — broader than specified but satisfies requirement.

acceptance_criteria_results.criterion: POST /api/conflicts/{id}/resolve with chosenObservationId not in conflict observation_ids returns 422.
acceptance_criteria_results.evidence: resolve/route.ts checks conflict.observationIds.includes(chosenObservationId), returns 422 OBSERVATION_NOT_IN_CONFLICT if not found.
resolve/route.ts checks role === 'viewer' early and returns 403 FORBIDDEN_ROLE.

acceptance_criteria_results.criterion: POST /api/observations/manual valid candidate payload returns 201 and persists Observation + AttestationRecord.
acceptance_criteria_results.evidence: manual/route.ts inserts attestationRecords and observations within withTenant transaction, returns 201 with { id, status, attestationRecordId }.
After transaction commit, checks result.status === 'approved' and calls semanticConflictQueue.add() with observationId and companyId.

acceptance_criteria_results.criterion: notification SSE event is delivered within 2 seconds of Notification insert.
acceptance_criteria_results.evidence: publish.ts performs DB insert immediately followed by Redis PUBLISH with no artificial delay. Code path is synchronous but 2-second SLA is a runtime property.
acceptance_criteria_results.notes: Requires integration/e2e test to formally verify the SLA.

acceptance_criteria_results.criterion: Notification bell badge increments on notification SSE without page reload.
acceptance_criteria_results.evidence: NotificationBell.tsx subscribes to EventSource at /api/pipeline/events. On 'notification' event, updates unreadCount and prepends notification to state. Badge renders reactively.

acceptance_criteria_results.criterion: PATCH /api/notifications/{id}/read returns 200 { read: true } and later unread query excludes it.
acceptance_criteria_results.evidence: read/route.ts sets read: true and returns { id, read: true }. GET /api/notifications with unread=true filters by eq(notifications.read, false), excluding read notifications.

acceptance_criteria_results.criterion: KeyEquivalenceCache prevents second Grok call for same key pair (verified by mock call counter).
acceptance_criteria_results.evidence: semanticConflict.ts queries keyEquivalenceCache by (companyId, keyPairHash). On cache hit, sets matchResult from cache and skips callGrokEquivalence. buildCacheKey sorts keys alphabetically for order-independent deduplication.
acceptance_criteria_results.notes: Mock call counter verification requires runtime test.

acceptance_criteria_results.criterion: TypeScript and test gates pass: tsc --noEmit and vitest run (plus relevant Playwright conflict/notification/manual flows).
acceptance_criteria_results.evidence: IDE reports some possible module resolution warnings but referenced modules exist on disk with correct exports. Actual tsc --noEmit and vitest results require execution.
acceptance_criteria_results.notes: LOW_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD mismatch (0.40 vs 0.70) will likely fail existing tests.

blocking_issues: C1: notification_type DB enum missing 'conflict_resolved' — conflict resolution notifications stored with wrong type, indistinguishable from detections
C2: Duplicate notification insert in POST /api/conflicts/{id}/resolve — in-transaction insert with wrong type + publishNotification after commit creates two rows
C3: Retry policy not wired — queue defaults missing attempts/backoff, transitions.ts enqueues with attempts:2/delay:1000 instead of attempts:3/backoff:2s/4s/8s
C4: LOW_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD = 0.40 instead of plan-required 0.70 — existing tests will fail
H1: attestation_records schema missing 'action' column — attestation action tracking entirely absent from data model
H2: Conflict case insert race condition — SELECT-then-INSERT instead of atomic ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING
H3: Malformed Grok responses incorrectly cached as DIFFERENT_KEY — permanently prevents valid re-classification
H4: notification_type enum missing 'pipeline_done' — remapped silently to 'pipeline_completed'
H5: ConflictDetectedEvent and NotificationEvent types in wrong file with extra 'id' field
recommended_fixes: Add migration to extend notification_type enum with 'conflict_resolved' and optionally 'pipeline_done'
Remove in-transaction notification insert from resolve/route.ts; rely solely on publishNotification after commit
Wire SEMANTIC_CONFLICT_RETRY_POLICY into semanticConflictQueue defaultJobOptions; fix transitions.ts to use attempts:3/backoff:2000
Add 'action' column to attestation_records via migration, or document this as a known deviation
Replace SELECT-then-INSERT for conflict cases with atomic INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING
Skip cache write when Grok response is malformed (check for PARSE_ERROR prefix in rationale)
Use publishNotification and publishPipelineEvent utilities in semanticConflict.ts instead of inline duplication
Include companyId in buildCacheKey hash to prevent cross-tenant cache sharing
Connect Zod schema to react-hook-form via zodResolver in ManualObservationForm
Add server-side initial fetch in conflicts/page.tsx for SSR
Cap limit parameter in GET /api/conflicts (e.g., max 100)
